function.name: browser_back
function.description: Navigate back to the previous page in browser history. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

function.name: browser_click
function.description: Click on an element identified by its ref ID from the snapshot (e.g., '@e5'). The ref IDs are shown in square brackets in the snapshot output. Requires browser_navigate and browser_snapshot to be called first.

ref.description: The element reference from the snapshot (e.g., '@e5', '@e12')

function.name: browser_console
function.description: Get browser console output and JavaScript errors from the current page. Returns console.log/warn/error/info messages and uncaught JS exceptions. Use this to detect silent JavaScript errors, failed API calls, and application warnings. Requires browser_navigate to be called first. When 'expression' is provided, evaluates JavaScript in the page context and returns the result — use this for DOM inspection, reading page state, or extracting data programmatically.

clear.description: If true, clear the message buffers after reading

expression.description: JavaScript expression to evaluate in the page context. Runs in the browser like DevTools console — full access to DOM, window, document. Return values are serialized to JSON. Example: 'document.title' or 'document.querySelectorAll("a").length'

function.name: browser_get_images
function.description: Get a list of all images on the current page with their URLs and alt text. Useful for finding images to analyze with the vision tool. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

function.name: browser_navigate
function.description: Navigate to a URL in the browser. Initializes the session and loads the page. Must be called before other browser tools. For simple information retrieval, prefer web_search or web_extract (faster, cheaper). For plain-text endpoints — URLs ending in .md, .txt, .json, .yaml, .yml, .csv, .xml, raw.githubusercontent.com, or any documented API endpoint — prefer curl via the terminal tool or web_extract; the browser stack is overkill and much slower for these. Use browser tools when you need to interact with a page (click, fill forms, dynamic content). Returns a compact page snapshot with interactive elements and ref IDs — no need to call browser_snapshot separately after navigating.

function.name: browser_press
function.description: Press a keyboard key. Useful for submitting forms (Enter), navigating (Tab), or keyboard shortcuts. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

function.name: browser_scroll
function.description: Scroll the page in a direction. Use this to reveal more content that may be below or above the current viewport. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

function.name: browser_snapshot
function.description: Get a text-based snapshot of the current page's accessibility tree. Returns interactive elements with ref IDs (like @e1, @e2) for browser_click and browser_type. full=false (default): compact view with interactive elements. full=true: complete page content. Snapshots over 8000 chars are truncated or LLM-summarized. Requires browser_navigate first. Note: browser_navigate already returns a compact snapshot — use this to refresh after interactions that change the page, or with full=true for complete content.

full.description: If true, returns complete page content. If false (default), returns compact view with interactive elements only.

function.name: browser_type
function.description: Type text into an input field identified by its ref ID. Clears the field first, then types the new text. Requires browser_navigate and browser_snapshot to be called first.

ref.description: The element reference from the snapshot (e.g., '@e3')

text.description: The text to type into the field

function.name: browser_vision
function.description: Take a screenshot of the current page and analyze it with vision AI. Use this when you need to visually understand what's on the page - especially useful for CAPTCHAs, visual verification challenges, complex layouts, or when the text snapshot doesn't capture important visual information. Returns both the AI analysis and a screenshot_path that you can share with the user by including MEDIA:<screenshot_path> in your response. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

question.description: What you want to know about the page visually. Be specific about what you're looking for.

annotate.description: If true, overlay numbered [N] labels on interactive elements. Each [N] maps to ref @eN for subsequent browser commands. Useful for QA and spatial reasoning about page layout.

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_local_search
function.description:
Brave Local Search API provides enrichments for location search results. Access to this API is available only through the Brave Search API Pro plans; confirm the user's plan before using this tool (if the user does not have a Pro plan, use the brave_web_search tool). Searches for local businesses and places using Brave's Local Search API. Best for queries related to physical locations, businesses, restaurants, services, etc.
    
    Returns detailed information including:
        - Business names and addresses
        - Ratings and review counts
        - Phone numbers and opening hours

    Use this when the query implies 'near me', 'in my area', or mentions specific locations (e.g., 'in San Francisco'). This tool automatically falls back to brave_web_search if no local results are found.

country.description: Search query country, where the results come from. The country string is limited to 2 character country codes of supported countries.

search_lang.description: Search language preference. The 2 or more character language code for which the search results are provided.

ui_lang.description: The language of the UI. The 2 or more character language code for which the search results are provided.

count.description: Number of results (1-20, default 10). Applies only to web search results (i.e., has no effect on locations, news, videos, etc.)

safesearch.description: Filters search results for adult content. The following values are supported: 'off' - No filtering. 'moderate' - Filters explicit content (e.g., images and videos), but allows adult domains in search results. 'strict' - Drops all adult content from search results. The default value is 'moderate'.

freshness.description: Filters search results by when they were discovered. The following values are supported: 'pd' - Discovered within the last 24 hours. 'pw' - Discovered within the last 7 days. 'YYYY-MM-DDtoYYYY-MM-DD' - Timeframe is also supported by specifying the date range e.g. [REDACTED_PHONE]to[REDACTED_PHONE].

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_news_search
function.description:
This tool searches for news articles using Brave's News Search API based on the user's query. Use it when you need current news information, breaking news updates, or articles about specific topics, events, or entities.
    
    When to use:
        - Finding recent news articles on specific topics
        - Getting breaking news updates
        - Researching current events or trending stories
        - Gathering news sources and headlines for analysis

    Returns a JSON list of news-related results with title, url, and description. Some results may contain snippets of text from the article.
    
    When relaying results in markdown-supporting environments, always cite sources with hyperlinks.
    
    Examples:
        - "According to [Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/technology/china-bans/), China bans uncertified and recalled power banks on planes".
        - "The [New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/2025/06/27/us/technology/ev-sales.html) reports that Tesla's EV sales have increased by 20%".
        - "According to [BBC News](https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-65910000), the UK government has announced a new policy to support renewable energy".

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_summarizer
function.description:
Retrieves AI-generated summaries of web search results using Brave's Summarizer API. This tool processes search results to create concise, coherent summaries of information gathered from multiple sources.

    When to use:

    - When you need a concise overview of complex topics from multiple sources
    - For quick fact-checking or getting key points without reading full articles

function.name: mcp_context7_query_docs
function.description:
Retrieves and queries up-to-date documentation and code examples from Context7 for any programming library or framework.

You must call 'Resolve Context7 Library ID' tool first to obtain the exact Context7-compatible library ID required to use this tool, UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question.

libraryId.description: Exact Context7-compatible library ID (e.g., '/mongodb/docs', '/vercel/next.js', '/supabase/supabase', '/vercel/next.js/v14.3.0-canary.87') retrieved from 'resolve-library-id' or directly from user query in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version'.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. Be specific and include relevant details. Good: 'How to set up authentication with JWT in Express.js' or 'React useEffect cleanup function examples'. Bad: 'auth' or 'hooks'. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

function.name: mcp_context7_resolve_library_id
function.description:
Resolves a package/product name to a Context7-compatible library ID and returns matching libraries.

You MUST call this function before 'Query Documentation' tool to obtain a valid Context7-compatible library ID UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Each result includes:
- Library ID: Context7-compatible identifier (format: /org/project)
- Name: Library or package name
- Description: Short summary
- Code Snippets: Number of available code examples
- Source Reputation: Authority indicator (High, Medium, Low, or Unknown)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)
- Versions: List of versions if available. Use one of those versions if the user provides a version in their query. The format of the version is /org/project/version.

For best results, select libraries based on name match, source reputation, snippet coverage, benchmark score, and relevance to your use case.

Selection Process:
1. Analyze the query to understand what library/package the user is looking for
2. Return the most relevant match based on:
- Name similarity to the query (exact matches prioritized)
- Description relevance to the query's intent
- Documentation coverage (prioritize libraries with higher Code Snippet counts)
- Source reputation (consider libraries with High or Medium reputation more authoritative)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)

Response Format:
- Return the selected library ID in a clearly marked section
- Provide a brief explanation for why this library was chosen
- If multiple good matches exist, acknowledge this but proceed with the most relevant one
- If no good matches exist, clearly state this and suggest query refinements

For ambiguous queries, request clarification before proceeding with a best-guess match.

IMPORTANT: Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question. If you cannot find what you need after 3 calls, use the best result you have.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. This is used to rank library results by relevance to what the user is trying to accomplish. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

libraryName.description: Library name to search for and retrieve a Context7-compatible library ID. Use the official library name with proper punctuation — e.g., 'Next.js' instead of 'nextjs', 'Customer.io' instead of 'customerio', 'Three.js' instead of 'threejs'.

function.name: [REDACTED_TOKEN]
function.description:
A detailed tool for dynamic and reflective problem-solving through thoughts.
This tool helps analyze problems through a flexible thinking process that can adapt and evolve.
Each thought can build on, question, or revise previous insights as understanding deepens.

When to use this tool:
- Breaking down complex problems into steps
- Planning and design with room for revision
- Analysis that might need course correction
- Problems where the full scope might not be clear initially
- Problems that require a multi-step solution
- Tasks that need to maintain context over multiple steps
- Situations where irrelevant information needs to be filtered out

Key features:
- You can adjust total_thoughts up or down as you progress
- You can question or revise previous thoughts
- You can add more thoughts even after reaching what seemed like the end
- You can express uncertainty and explore alternative approaches
- Not every thought needs to build linearly - you can branch or backtrack
- Generates a solution hypothesis
- Verifies the hypothesis based on the Chain of Thought steps
- Repeats the process until satisfied
- Provides a correct answer

Parameters explained:
- thought: Your current thinking step, which can include:
  * Regular analytical steps
  * Revisions of previous thoughts
  * Questions about previous decisions
  * Realizations about needing more analysis
  * Changes in approach
  * Hypothesis generation
  * Hypothesis verification
- nextThoughtNeeded: True if you need more thinking, even if at what seemed like the end
- thoughtNumber: Current number in sequence (can go beyond initial total if needed)
- totalThoughts: Current estimate of thoughts needed (can be adjusted up/down)
- isRevision: A boolean indicating if this thought revises previous thinking
- revisesThought: If is_revision is true, which thought number is being reconsidered
- branchFromThought: If branching, which thought number is the branching point
- branchId: Identifier for the current branch (if any)
- needsMoreThoughts: If reaching end but realizing more thoughts needed

You should:
1. Start with an initial estimate of needed thoughts, but be ready to adjust
2. Feel free to question or revise previous thoughts
3. Don't hesitate to add more thoughts if needed, even at the "end"
4. Express uncertainty when present
5. Mark thoughts that revise previous thinking or branch into new paths
6. Ignore information that is irrelevant to the current step
7. Generate a solution hypothesis when appropriate
8. Verify the hypothesis based on the Chain of Thought steps
9. Repeat the process until satisfied with the solution
10. Provide a single, ideally correct answer as the final output
11. Only set nextThoughtNeeded to false when truly done and a satisfactory answer is reached

nextThoughtNeeded.description: Whether another thought step is needed

totalThoughts.description: Estimated total thoughts needed (numeric value, e.g., 5, 10)

1. Website performance issues (slow loading, broken pages, mobile problems)
2. Content gaps — what don't they cover that clients need?
3. Pricing weaknesses — hidden fees, confusing tiers, no transparency
4. Language gaps — who only serves one language?
5. UX/design weaknesses — outdated sites, poor mobile, hard to navigate
6. Missing features — no chat, no WhatsApp, no calculator, no self-service
7. Trust signals — fake logos, missing team info, no real testimonials
8. Social proof — poor reviews, low social media following

Load 3-4 competitor sites and audit them. Take notes on specific flaws Nexa can beat them on.
I'll audit 4 major competitors: weparaguay.com, paraguaysimply.com, paraguaysovereign.com, and destination-paraguay.com. Let me start loading them.

Paraguay residency is a powerful tool for freedom, but it’s not a loophole. We take our reputation and our clients’ legal safety seriously.

FAQ’s

## Frequently asked question

Why should I choose Paraguay for residency?

Paraguay offers one of the world’s simplest and most affordable residency options, with no minimum investment requirements, a territorial tax system (0% on foreign income), and fast-track processing in as little as 2–3 months. It’s a perfect fit for digital nomads, entrepreneurs, and investors looking for freedom without bureaucracy.

Read More: Paraguay Residency: The Ultimate Freedom Strategy

How does Paraguay compare to other popular residency programs like Portugal or Dubai?

Compared to Portugal’s current Golden Visa regulations or Dubai’s business setup, Paraguay remains the most accessible and flexible option in 2026.

While Portugal has eliminated the real estate investment path and now primarily requires €500,000+ in specific private equity or venture capital funds with long processing wait times.Dubai continues to demand high annual maintenance fees and strict physical office compliance.

In contrast, Paraguay offers a legal residency path for a fraction of the cost, with significantly faster processing and no requirement to physically relocate. It provides the ultimate "Plan B" for those who want global mobility without the half-million-euro price tag or the bureaucratic red tape.

Read more: Why Paraguay Beats Portugal’s Golden Visa Also see: Paraguay vs Dubai for Digital Nomads

Can I really pay 0% taxes as a Paraguay resident?

Yes. Paraguay uses a territorial tax system, which means only income generated inside Paraguay is taxed. Foreign income (including online business, investments, or crypto trading) is completely tax-free if structured correctly.

Read more: Paraguay Residency for Crypto Investors: Tax-Free Guide

How do I maintain my Paraguay residency status?

Maintaining your residency in Paraguay is remarkably simple compared to most global programs. To keep your status active, you only need to visit the country once every three years for at least 24 hours. There are no requirements to live in the country, pay local social security (unless you are working locally), or spend a minimum number of months per year in the territory. This flexibility makes it the ideal "Plan B" for global entrepreneurs who need a secure residency while remaining mobile.

Read more: Can You Lose Paraguay Residency? Maintenance Requirements

Can WeParaguay also help with U.S. business setup?

Absolutely. We specialize in creating a comprehensive global structure for our clients. Beyond securing your residency, we can establish a U.S. LLC for you, including obtaining your EIN (Tax ID) and setting up U.S. business banking.

This dual setup allows you to operate globally with the prestige of a U.S. company while enjoying the tax-friendly environment and flexibility of your Paraguayan residency. It’s the ultimate setup for digital nomads, consultants, and e-commerce entrepreneurs.

Learn more:

- View our U.S. Business Solutions page
- Read our guide: Why a U.S. LLC + Paraguay Residency is the ultimate power move

Can I open a U.S. company while living in Paraguay?

Yes. Many Paraguay residents set up U.S. LLCs to access international markets, Stripe/PayPal, and strong U.S. banking. The combination of 0% Paraguay taxes with U.S. company credibility makes this one of the most powerful setups in the world.

Read more: Paraguay + U.S. LLC Tax Strategy

What are the hidden costs of getting residency in Paraguay?

At WeParaguay, we don't believe in "hidden" costs, but there are external expenses that every applicant should budget for beyond our service fee. Here is exactly what to expect:

Document Preparation in Your Home Country: You will need to pay for the issuance of your birth certificate and clean criminal record, plus the Apostille and any local notary fees. These prices vary by country.

Flights & Accommodation: You must visit Asunción for at least one week to file your application in person. Your travel and stay are not included in our service fee.

Entry Visa (If applicable): Depending on your citizenship, you might need a visa to enter Paraguay. If so, there is a small fee ($20–$160) to verify your visa at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs upon arrival.

Our Promise: Our service fee covers the entire legal process, government filing fees, and professional handling. We will provide you with a clear checklist so you know exactly what every dollar is spent on before you even book your flight.

How long until I can get Paraguayan citizenship and a passport?

Paraguay offers one of the fastest naturalization timelines in the world. After 3 years of permanent residency, you can apply for citizenship and a Paraguayan passport, which grants visa-free access to 140+ countries and Mercosur benefits.

Once you submit the citizenship application after the 5-year mark, the court process typically takes 6 to 12 months to be finalized. Once approved, you receive your Naturalization Certificate, which allows you to apply for your Paraguayan Passport (issued within 30 days).

Read more: Paraguay Citizenship Timeline

What tax traps should digital nomads avoid?

The most dangerous trap today is the "Perpetual Tourist" myth—traveling constantly without a legal tax home. In 2026, under new reporting standards like CRS 2.0 and CARF, banks and crypto exchanges (like Binance or Kraken) now automatically flag users without a clear Tax ID (TIN) as "High Risk." This leads to frozen accounts and aggressive audits.

Another common trap is the "183-Day Rule" misconception; many nomads think staying under 6 months in a country makes them tax-exempt, but authorities often use "Center of Vital Interests" tests to claim you as a resident anyway.

The Paraguay Solution: We help you avoid these traps by establishing a legitimate tax residency. Unlike "paper-only" providers, we don't just get you an ID card; we manage your monthly tax filings (RUC/IVA). This creates the official government paper trail that Tier-1 banks demand to prove you have a compliant tax home in a territorial tax jurisdiction.

Learn more:

Read the full guide: Why Traditional Nomad Tax Strategies Fail in 2026

Discover our Paraguay + U.S. LLC "Power Move" Strategy

How do I open a bank account in Paraguay?

To open an account in 2026, you must first obtain your Paraguayan Cédula (ID). Banks strictly require this for both personal and business accounts. Once your ID is issued, you will need to provide proof of local tax registration (RUC), a local address certificate, and documented proof of your source of funds. We recommend starting with a digital-first bank like Ueno for speed, before moving to Tier-1 institutions like Itaú for international operations.

Read more: Paraguay Banking Guide

How does Paraguay compare to Panama for residency?

While both countries offer excellent territorial tax systems (0% tax on foreign income), Paraguay is the clear winner for affordability and simplicity in 2026. Panama’s popular "Friendly Nations Visa" now requires a significant $200,000 investment (in real estate or a bank deposit) or local employment to qualify. In contrast, Paraguay requires $0 investment for temporary residency, you only pay the processing fees. Paraguay also has a faster path to citizenship (3 years of permanent residency vs. 5 years in Panama) and a much lower cost of living.

Read more: Paraguay vs Panama Residency

Is Paraguay good for crypto investors?

Yes, Paraguay is currently one of the most crypto-friendly jurisdictions in the world. Due to its territorial tax system, capital gains from crypto trading and offshore exchanges are generally taxed at 0%, as long as the income is considered foreign-sourced. Additionally, Paraguay has become a global hub for crypto mining thanks to its abundance of inexpensive hydroelectric power. However, with the 2026 global implementation of CARF (Crypto-Asset Reporting Framework), having a formal tax residency certificate is now essential to satisfy exchange KYC and protect your assets.

Read more: Paraguay Residency for Crypto Investors

Why is Paraguay residency considered the ultimate “Plan B”?

In 2026, Paraguay is the top choice for a "Plan B" because it offers maximum freedom with minimum obligation. It is one of the few countries that does not require a large investment or a permanent physical presence, you only need to visit once every three years to keep your status active. Combined with a territorial tax system (0% tax on foreign income) and a very low cost of living, it serves as a secure, affordable legal fortress that protects your mobility and your assets without tying you down to one location.

Read more: Paraguay Residency: The Ultimate Freedom Strategy

What if my residency process fails?

At WeParaguay, we minimize risk by thoroughly reviewing your documents before submission.As a registered Paraguayan company (WeParaguay E.A.S.), we operate with defined procedures and professional accountability.Every case is handled carefully to ensure full compliance before it reaches the migration desk.

Can I live anywhere?

Yes, and this is exactly why it is the ultimate "Plan B." While you have the legal right to live and work in Paraguay, you are not required to stay there. To maintain your status in 2026, you simply need to visit the country at least once every three years for a minimum of 24 hours. This flexibility allows you to travel the world or live in other countries while keeping a secure, tax-efficient legal base in South America.

{
  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://paraguaysovereign.com/pricing/",
      "title": "Paraguay Residency Costs 2026 | From $2,290, No Hidden Fees | Paraguay Sovereign",
      "content": "Paraguay Residency Costs 2026 | From $2,290, No Hidden Fees | Paraguay Sovereign

Quick Answer

Paraguay residency packages range from $2,290 to $5,490 depending on support level and case complexity. No bank deposit is required for standard residency since October 2022. Case complexity (family size, document errors, nationality requirements) affects total engagement cost. Professional support prevents document rework that adds significant cost and delays.

Updated May 2026 | Source: Dirección General de Migraciones[1]

#### Transparent Pricing, No Hidden Fees

Our residency packages are comprehensive with transparent pricing. We believe in clarity about what's included-and what could increase your costs if not handled correctly. Document errors and rework are the largest hidden cost in residency applications.

## Residency Packages

Essential

$2,290

$2,176 with crypto

- • Core residency support
- • Document review
- • Government liaison
- • All translations

Premium (Most Popular)

$3,190

$3,030 with crypto

- • RUC tax registration
- • 12 months RUC filing
- • Banking introduction

Investor

$5,490

$5,215 with crypto

- • SUACE investor route
- • Immediate permanent
- • Company formation
- • Tax strategy consult

View detailed package comparison →

## What Changes Total Engagement Cost

While residency packages have standard pricing, several factors can increase your total engagement cost through additional support, extended timelines, or document rework.

### Case Complexity Factors

- • Family size: Each family member requires separate application processing
- • Document errors: Incorrect apostilles or expired documents require re-preparation
- • Nationality requirements: Non-Hague countries require consular legalization
- • Name changes: Marriage, divorce, or legal name changes require additional documentation
- • Multi-country residency: Background checks from multiple countries of residence

### Timeline Extension Costs

- • Additional travel: Returning to Paraguay for Cédula collection or re-submission
- • Extended accommodation: Longer stays due to processing delays
- • Expedited services: Priority processing for time-sensitive cases
- • Storage and forwarding: Document holding and international shipping

### Additional Service Needs

- • Tax residency setup: RUC registration and tax ID establishment
- • Banking introduction: Account opening assistance and documentation
- • Company formation: SUACE investor route and entity setup
- • Legal consultations: Specialized advice for complex situations

## Complexity Tiers: Simple, Standard, and Complex Cases

Understanding your case complexity tier helps estimate the appropriate service level and potential additional costs.

### Simple Cases

Typical profile: Single applicant, standard documents, no complications

- • Single adult with straightforward document history
- • Hague Convention country with standard apostille process
- • No name changes or marital status complications
- • Documents in good condition with consistent information

Service recommendation: Essential or Premium package sufficient

### Standard Cases

Typical profile: Family applications or minor complications

- • Married couples or families with children
- • Documents from multiple countries requiring coordination
- • Name changes requiring marriage or divorce certificates
- • Some documents need re-preparation or correction

Service recommendation: Premium package recommended

### Complex Cases

Typical profile: Significant complications or specialized requirements

- • Non-Hague Convention countries requiring consular legalization
- • Multiple countries of residence requiring background checks from each
- • Significant document errors requiring complete re-preparation
- • Investor or business immigration requiring SUACE and company formation
- • Time-sensitive cases requiring expedited processing

Service recommendation: Investor package recommended for comprehensive support

## Value of Error Prevention

The largest hidden cost in residency applications is document error and rework. Professional service prevents these costs through validation and proper preparation.

#### Document Re-Prevention Costs

Avoided Cost: $200-800 per document for re-apostille

Prevention Value: Professional document review identifies apostille authority errors before submission, avoiding expensive re-documentation.

#### Additional Travel Costs

Avoided Cost: $1,000-2,500 for return trip to collect Cédula

Prevention Value: Proper document preparation and remote Cédula collection (Premium/Investor packages) eliminate the need to return specifically for Cédula collection. Note: You must still visit Paraguay at least once every 12 months to maintain your residency status.

#### Timeline Extension Costs

Avoided Cost: Extended accommodation, delayed life plans, opportunity cost

Prevention Value: Getting documents right the first time prevents months of delay and extended accommodation expenses.

#### Application Rejection Risk

Risk without professional service: 15-25% rejection rate for DIY applications

Prevention Value: Professional service reduces rejection risk to under 2% through comprehensive document validation and proper submission.

#### ✅ Our Approach to Cost Management

We include comprehensive document validation in all residency packages to prevent the expensive rework that increases total engagement cost. By identifying issues before you travel, we save significantly more than our fee in avoided re-documentation, additional travel, and timeline delays.

Discuss your case complexity →

#### No Bank Deposit Required

Paraguay eliminated the $5,000 bank deposit requirement in October 2022 under Law 6984. No bank deposit or investment is required for residency, making Paraguay one of the most affordable residency programs globally.

## Hidden Costs to Avoid

These are the hidden costs that increase total engagement when working with low-quality providers or attempting DIY applications:

#### Document Rework Expenses

Incorrect apostille authorities, expired documents, or inconsistent information require complete re-documentation at $200-800 per document.

#### Additional Travel Costs

Rejection or document issues requiring personal return to Paraguay cost $1,000-2,500 in flights, accommodation, and time.

#### Service Markups

Some providers charge excessive markups on translation services, government fees, or document processing-often 3-5x actual cost.

#### Timeline Delay Costs

Extended timelines mean delayed life plans, additional accommodation expenses, and opportunity costs from postponed relocation or business setup.

## Payment Timing & Options

#### Our Payment Schedule

1

##### 50% Deposit

Due when you book. This begins your document review and preparation phase.

2

##### 50% Balance

Due before your Paraguay trip, once all documents are prepared and verified.

Bank Transfer

USD or EUR wire

Credit Card

Cryptocurrency

5% Discount

## Frequently Asked Questions

### How much does Paraguay residency cost?

Paraguay residency packages range from $2,290 to $5,490 depending on support level and case complexity. No bank deposit is required for standard residency since October 2022.

### What factors increase total engagement cost?

Case complexity factors increase costs: family size (each member separate application), document errors requiring re-preparation, nationality requirements (non-Hague consular legalization), name changes requiring additional documentation, and multi-country background checks.

### What is included in Paraguay residency packages?

All packages include document review, certified translations, application submission, government appointment accompaniment, and Cédula collection coordination. Higher tiers add concierge services, expedited processing, tax residency setup, and company formation.

### How does family pricing work?

Each family member requires a separate application and full package price. Family cases are typically standard complexity tier due to additional coordination and documentation requirements.

### Why does case complexity matter?

Case complexity determines appropriate service level and potential additional costs. Simple cases (single adult, standard documents) need less support. Complex cases (family, document errors, non-Hague countries) benefit from higher-tier packages to prevent expensive rework and delays.

### What is the payment schedule for residency packages?

We require 50% deposit to begin document preparation, with remaining 50% due before your Paraguay trip once documents are verified. We accept bank transfer, credit card, or cryptocurrency (5% discount).

### What if my application is rejected-do I get a refund?

Rejections are rare (under 2%) with proper documentation. If rejected due to government error or circumstances beyond control, we provide partial refund minus costs incurred. If rejected due to document issues despite our guidance, we work to fix the problem at no additional service fee.

### Are there any ongoing annual costs after getting residency?

Minimal. Temporary residency requires renewal every 2 years. Permanent residency has no renewal requirement. Optional costs include annual tax filing if you have local income and health insurance if desired.

      "url": "https://paraguaypathways.com/services/residency/",
      "title": "Residency by Investment in Paraguay: How It Works in 2024",
      "content": "Residency by Investment in Paraguay: How It Works in 2024

Paraguay offers one of the most accessible residency by investment programs available. Unlike the more traditional process, which requires obtaining temporary residency before permanent residency, the residency by investment program allows immediate access to permanent residency valid for 10 years. Additionally, family members of those who obtain residency through investment can also gain permanent residency without going through the temporary stage, another interesting advantage to consider with this type of process.

Over the years, we have helped more than 80 people from around the world obtain residency through investment, and 250+ people worldwide to obtain their residency in Paraguay through the more classic process involving temporary residency first. If you are considering doing the same and need assistance, you can send us an email: our team will be happy to help you, and we respond to all inquiries within 24 hours.

## How does the residency by investment program in Paraguay work?

The residency by investment program in Paraguay is designed to attract individuals who can encourage the creation of opportunities and jobs, as established by Law No. 978/96. Unlike other similar programs around the world, the applicant can achieve the status of resident by investment even before making the actual investment. The important thing is to demonstrate the capacity to invest in the country, as required.

To access this program, it is necessary for SUACE, the Paraguayan entity managing relations between public administration and private companies, to recognize the applicant the constancia de inversionista. This is a document in which SUACE certifies that the applicant is qualified as an investor. To qualify for the constancia de inversionista, the applicant shall present a business plan to SUACE showing an investment project of at least $70,000 and the subsequent creation of at least 5 jobs.

Our company also provides support for those wishing to obtain the invitation letter as an investor, which is needed for Citizens of countries that require a visa to travel to Paraguay. If you are interested, you can send us an email and talk for free with our team about your specific situation.

## Requirements for obtaining residency by investment

The documents required to obtain residency through investment, as stipulated by Law No. 6984/2022, are:

- Entry visa to the country, if required;
- The certificate of constancia de inversionista, which we just discussed;
- Personal or company bank account with at least $70,000 in liquidity;
- Passport with at least 6 months of validity before expiration;
- Apostilled birth certificate;
- Apostilled background check;
- Apostilled certificate proving your civil status (marriage, divorce, or death certificate of spouse);
- Certificate issued by the Department of Computer Science of Paraguay stating that the applicant has not committed cybercrimes;
- Certificate issued by Paraguay’s Interpol attesting to the absence of ongoing legal proceedings against the applicant;
- Spanish translation, performed by a public translator registered in the official roster, of all documents;

The last three documents must be obtained directly in Paraguay.

## How long does it take to obtain residency through investment?

The time required to obtain residency through investment in Paraguay varies significantly depending on whether the procedures are carried out independently or with the help of a consultant.

If you choose to do the process with the help of a manager, it will take 5 working days. This is the expected process:

- Upon arrival in Paraguay, the manager should have already had all documents translated by a public translator registered in the official roster;
- In the first 3-4 days, you will carry out all initial procedures (Interpol documents, Department of Computer Science, and the identification card of the Paraguayan national police);
- On the 5th day, you are ready to apply for the carnet de radicaccion;
- Once the client has returned to their home country, the manager will take care of receiving the carnet de radicaccion and requesting the identity card on behalf of their client. If necessary, they will also take care of opening a bank account and the RUC number, and then send all the documents where the client desires to receive them.

This is, at least, what we offer to our clients. The experience and time required may vary depending on the manager you rely on.

In the case of doing everything on your own, it will take 45-60 days, of which:

- In the first 3-4 days, one waits for the authenticated translation of their documents;
- From the 5th to the 9th day, you will conduct the initial procedures;
- On the 10th day, you are ready to go to the Department of Immigration to request the carnet de radicaccion;
- Between the 25th and 35th day, the carnet de radicaccion should be ready. With this, you can apply for the ID card;
- In the following 15-20 days, the ID card should also be ready;
- At this point, count another 3-4 days to open a bank account, apply for a driving license if desired, and request the RUC number if desired.

### Maintaining and renewing permanent residency

Despite the name, “permanent” residency actually needs to be renewed every 10 years. The process is very simple and requires just a visit to the Department of Immigration of the National Police.

There are no annual costs to maintain residency, and no minimum stay is required. Just know that the amount of time you spend in Paraguay may have some implications on your tax situation. You can discover more about this in our guide to minimum stay requirements.

As established by article 34 of Law No. 978/96, it is possible to lose residency if a person qualified as an investor does not actually complete their investment within 90 days following the issuance of the constancia de inversionista document.

## Residency by investment in Paraguay: what are the alternatives?

The main alternative to the residency by investment program is to first obtain temporary residency and then convert it to permanent residency. This alternative does not require committing to an investment in the country and does not require to show your bank account, granting lower costs and higher flexibility.

Temporary residency is valid for 2 years, and you are eligible to convert it to permanent residency after just 12 months. This step only requires a visit to an office of the Department of Immigration and is very simple.

Read also: Complete guide to Temporary Residency in Paraguay

The advantages of residency by investment are:

- Immediate obtaining of permanent residency;
- Facilitating the opening of a company in Paraguay;
- Avoiding the conversion of temporary residency to permanent, which can save a trip to Paraguay for the applicant.

The advantages of first obtaining temporary residency are:

- No need to present a business plan, also avoiding the cost and time needed to prepare it;
- Not having to commit to investing in the country;
- No requirement to demonstrate one’s assets or income.

Both paths are equally effective for eventually obtaining the Paraguayan passport 3 years after obtaining residency in the country.

## Need help with residency procedures?

If you need help with residency in Paraguay, feel free to send us an email. We are always happy to answer all questions and doubts, which we do within 24 hours even on holidays. Our team responds in English, Spanish, Portuguese, and Italian. Moreover, we can offer, through our accountants and lawyers, consultations on taxation and investments in Paraguay.

We are pleased to say that the Paraguay Pathways team has already helped hundreds of foreign citizens obtain their permanent residency in Paraguay, both through investment and through the classic route of temporary residency.",

Here's a summary of what I did and found:

## What I Did

Loaded and audited **8 competitor sites**, deeply analyzing **6** (weparaguay.com, paraguaysimply.com, paraguaysovereign.com, destination-paraguay.com, paraguaypathways.com, lowcostparaguay.com) plus scanned 2 more (paraguayeasyresidency.com, terravida.polsia.app). - **paraguaysimply.com**: Residency options page = 404

**paraguaypathways.com** is the strongest — full team page, 7 languages, 750+ clients, active blog, real social media presence. But they hide all pricing and lack WhatsApp/chat/calculator.

**lowcostparaguay.com** ($350 base fee) competes on price alone but has zero trust signals, no team, no social proof, and English-only. Nexa can beat them on quality + trust + multilingual while being competitive on price.
